name: it-review
description: Review the SOW/service design/runbook (breadth then depth) and fix findings until no Blocker or Major remains.
rubric: The breadth pass scanned the current draft for missing sections, readiness gates without verdicts, and unsubstantiated claims.
The depth pass returned an explicit verdict for each of the five gates (Scope Clarity, SLA Measurability, Operational Readiness, Security & Compliance, Cost/Effort Traceability).
Zero Blocker findings remain (e.g., a missing Rollback section, an unmeasurable SLA, a readiness gate with no verdict).
Zero Major findings remain (e.g., a scope item that is neither in nor out of scope, a cost line traced to no scoped work).
The final sign-off is consistent with the scoped work and the agreed SLA targets.
fixPrompt: Each iteration: (1) breadth-review the current deliverable for completeness and internal consistency; (2) depth-review by issuing a verdict on each gate in gates.md; (3) if no Blocker/Major findings remain and it-validate is green, converge — report Minor/Nit findings without iterating; (4) otherwise fix the Blocker/Major findings in the deliverable and re-run it-validate before the next pass.
guardrails: Findings are fixed in the deliverable, not argued away — a gate failure means the design changes, not the verdict text.
Do not soften an SLA target or drop a security control to clear a gate.
Re-review the fixes themselves each iteration, not just the prior findings list.
onExhausted: Stop. Report the remaining Blocker/Major findings, the fix attempted for each, and recommend human delivery-lead review or a return to the Design stage.
